answer:
The electric field at point (1, 3, -1) is \( \mathbf{E} = -1\,\mathbf{a}_x + 3\,\mathbf{a}_y + 3\,\mathbf{a}_z \).  
For a positive test charge, the force is in the direction of \(\mathbf{E}\); maximum opposing force occurs when moving opposite to \(\mathbf{E}\), and maximum aiding force when moving along \(\mathbf{E}\).  
The unit vector in the direction of \(\mathbf{E}\) is \(\frac{-1\mathbf{a}_x + 3\mathbf{a}_y + 3\mathbf{a}_z}{\sqrt{19}} \approx -0.229\mathbf{a}_x + 0.688\mathbf{a}_y + 0.688\mathbf{a}_z\), so the opposing direction is its negative, matching option H.

\boxed{H}
